key_insight: The paper proposes a speculative materialist ontology in which consciousness is treated as an intrinsic property of physical assembly and information conservation across all scales of matter.
engineering_relevance: For coating, welding, and machine-design contexts, the abstract mainly suggests a metaphorical framework linking microstructural cohesion, density evolution, and information retention rather than a testable materials model.
method: Defines a non-reductive physicalist framework built around intrinsic consciousness and constructivist material organization.
Interprets thoughts and biological change as physical emissions that alter material density and state.
Introduces laws of informational permanence based on structural cohesion, quasi-perfection, and post-biological evolution.
evidence_signal: The abstract cites prior DOI-linked works and a 2008-to-2026 development timeline, but it provides no experimental dataset, simulation, or validation metrics.
watch_item: The claims are highly speculative and do not present a conventional engineering methodology, so they should not be treated as validated materials-science evidence.
paper_title: QUÉ ES LA CONSCIENCIA. Hacia un Monismo Materialista Constructivista: El Entramado Absoluto de Consciencias Intrínsecas, Conservación Informática y Dinámica de Densidades en el Ecosistema Universal (2008–2026)
